signals.reasoning: Cover the live MON short. It has only retained about 14% of peak P&L, the giveback warning is urgent, and the board is no longer bearish enough to justify defending the remaining scraps under veto conditions.

Cover the live LIT short. It has retained only about 5% of peak P&L, funding is strongly positive but the realized edge has already evaporated, and the urgent giveback warning makes this a protect-what-is-left exit.
BTC remains stable but the active veto keeps benchmark exposure in watch mode rather than action mode.
ETH does not offer enough directional clarity to justify a new position under the current event brake.
SOL still has mildly constructive structure, but veto keeps it below the threshold for a fresh long.
DASH screens bullish with negative funding and strong mechanics, but the event veto should keep even strong continuation names in watch mode for now.
COMP has one of the strongest squeeze-style long profiles on the board, but the veto state should keep it out of the buy bucket despite the attractive mechanics.
CHILLGUY remains stretched and crowded, but veto means this is watch-only rather than an active fade.
BLUR is weak with negative funding, which makes it noisy rather than cleanly bearish or bullish, so it stays sidelined.
DYM remains the cleanest bearish name on the rest of the board because price is weak and the mechanical bias is firmly negative, though conviction is capped by the veto regime.
MORPHO has momentum but not enough structural edge or execution quality to justify action here.
